AGT Pipeline Conditions for 4/22/2026


Notice Text:

For Gas Day April 22, 2026, Algonquin Gas Transmission (AGT) has approved and scheduled nominations at each pipeline segment and meter station up to AGT's operational capacity. The following locations have been restricted due to requested nominations exceeding AGT's operational capacity:












Restricted Locations Scheduled and Sealed Priority % Restricted   Notes
AO IT 3B 3A 2C 2B 2A 1
Stony Point
100% 100% 80%




 
Cromwell   100% 100% 66%            
Burrillville
100% 100% 82%





Weymouth   100% 100% 98%            
Iroquois Deliveries
100% 100% 64%





Iroquois Receipts   100% 100% 57%            
Hanover West
100% 100% 21%





Mendon Receipts   100% 100% 100% 100% 44%          
G System   100% 100% 45%            
C System Yes                    
Deliveries to Texas Eastern   100% 100% 100% 100% 87%       Planned Outage






















No-Notice Restrictions ---





















OFO / Imbalance Notice










System Wide OFO ---









Customer Specific OFO ---









Meter Specific OFO ---









Customer Specific Hourly OFO ---









Imbalances Warnings Stay Balanced





















FERC Order 698 Yes





















Systemwide Notes:










Due to the planned Hanover station maintenance, beginning Gas Day April 20, 2025, AGT will restrict 100% of 3B nominations delivering to Texas Eastern (TE).  In addition, restrictions to 3A and 2C nominations delivering to TE may be necessary for future Gas Days.

(1) AGT has scheduled Transco Centerville receipts to the design meter capacity of 222,526 Dth/d.   
AGT requests that customers/point operators on AGT be aware of the impact non-ratable hourly takes from the system may have in causing delivery pressures reaching lower than desired levels.












AGT's system is not designed to sustain delivery pressures above contract levels while making non-ratable/accelerated deliveries above scheduled quantities for more than 6 consecutive hours, to be followed by flows below scheduled quantity for the balance of any 24-hour period. If customers/point operators don't manage hourly takes from the system, 1) delivery pressures will be impacted and /or 2) AGT may be required to impose further restrictions or courses of action in order to maintain the operational integrity of the system.












AGT may be required to issue system specific, meter specific and/or hourly OFO's pursuant to General Terms and Conditions Section 26.7(d) to impose further restrictions in order to maintain the operational integrity of the system.












Customers are advised that capacity may become available as the nomination and confirmation process continues throughout the day.

Please contact your Operations Account Representative should you have any questions.
